select_when: Delivery speed and reliability have to coexist, and the bottleneck is integration friction rather than lack of ideas.
Failures are being created by handoffs, untested merges, delayed feedback, brittle release steps, or poor coordination between builders and operators.
The system needs a living operating loop that shortens the distance between build, observe, diagnose, and adjust.
Local throughput gains may be degrading end-to-end service, so the work needs system-level rather than step-local reasoning.
avoid_when: Pipeline optimization is being treated as an abstract slogan without specific failure paths, rollback realities, or operator constraints.
The team is using speed metrics to hide system fragility, debugging latency, or rollback pain.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on integration friction, living operating loops, and end-to-end reliability rather than software-fashion branding.
donor_drops: Did not widen into broad platform-engineering process language because the raw source is strongest on feedback loops, integration pain, and local-versus-system optimization.
open_questions: The source is synthesized from adjacent materials rather than a narrow technical manual, so the operational labels stay conservative.

select_when.note: The strengths section explicitly names coexistence of speed and reliability, integration friction, living loops, and end-to-end learning.

avoid_when.note: Both avoid conditions come directly from the anti-pattern language about oversimplified abstraction and local optimization hiding fragility.

input_type.note: The source activates on failures created by integration and handoff friction, while the compact label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook yields continuous build-observe-adjust loops; the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model reasons about system interactions and consequence chains, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
